input.plan:
# Homepage redesign — Warm Horizon

## Context

[`design.md`](design.md) already locks the system: **modern-minimal**, marketing macrostructure **feature-stack / long-document**, theme **Warm Horizon** (ivory paper, sky surfaces, logo navy `#0e3a69`, orange `#ff6b35`, Lexend + Manrope).

[`homepage/app/design-tokens.css`](homepage/app/design-tokens.css) and [`homepage/app/globals.css`](homepage/app/globals.css) already implement that palette and CTA voice. Sibling routes (`how-it-works`, `pricing`) already match. The landing page is **token-compliant but compositionally flat**: hero → benefits → faux board → thin trust rows → duplicate search CTA. Glow hooks are neutralized; proof chips risk invented metrics (`Germany · 42 roles`).

This plan is a **Hallmark redesign** (new rhythm + copy + enrichment) inside existing routes/components — no route deletion, no token system rewrite.

## Design intent (locked)

- **Voice:** open door — warm light, clear guidance, act without shouting (`design.md` genre).
- **Brand signal:** KUCHUP wordmark + bird remain hero-level (not nav-only).
- **Keep product core:** live `SearchBar` + `SearchFlowProvider` stay on the page (search is the product, not decoration).
- **CTA voice:** orange primary pill + sky-outline secondary; tactile press from existing `.btn-primary`.
- **Honesty:** no invented stats. Drop or replace fabricated role counts; keep only claims we can defend (`updated every 6 hours`, real country list, ATS platforms if already documented).
- **Enrichment:** marketing Tier-A (CSS horizon wash) + existing Tier-B bird SVG. Orange-only atmosphere — no multi-hue glows, no Hum pear/cyan/coral.

## Target section rhythm

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  Header[Header nav-glass]
  Hero[Hero brand plus search]
  Results[SearchResults conditional]
  Benefits[Benefits three pillars]
  Board[Product board preview]
  Trust[Single trust band]
  Close[Closing CTA statement]
  Footer[Footer]

  Header --> Hero --> Results --> Benefits --> Board --> Trust --> Close --> Footer
```

1. **Hero** — brand-first open composition: Lexend H1, short lede, primary/secondary CTAs, then search. Desktop keeps bird; add a soft sky/horizon wash behind the mark (Tier-A CSS, tokens only).
2. **SearchResults** — unchanged behavior (appears after search).
3. **Benefits** — same three product pillars, rewritten copy; asymmetric grid kept; remove invented chip numbers.
4. **Board preview** — `#board` product screenshot; clean dead purple-glow hook; light orange/sky atmosphere only.
5. **Trust band** — merge `ReassuranceStrip` + `ProofFooter` into one calm band (honest bullets, no fake metrics).
6. **Closing CTA** — statement-style close (open door to panel), **one** search instance max here or defer to hero-only to avoid duplicate chrome; prefer primary “Open the board” + secondary “Sign in”, with search only if hero search is scrolled away (keep one SearchBar in hero as primary).

## Copy direction (theme-led, not verbatim dump)

Rewrite for Warm Horizon tone — sample direction (final strings set during implementation):

| Spot | From | Toward |
|------|------|--------|
| Hero H1 | “All your opportunities, in one place” | Door/open-sky framing, e.g. “Your next country starts with one board” |
| Eyebrow | “Relocation-friendly engineering roles” | Shorter brand cue tied to visa-friendly EU engineering |
| Benefits H2 | “What you get” | Outcome voice, e.g. “Built for the move” |
| CTA | “Ready to search?” | “Step through” / open-the-board close |

Preserve factual product claims: company career-page scrape cadence, tracking buckets, CV reframe with user approval, no spam applications.

## Files to change (in place)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| [`homepage/app/page.tsx`](homepage/app/page.tsx) | Reorder/wrap sections; drop duplicate thin strips once merged |
| [`homepage/components/Hero.tsx`](homepage/components/Hero.tsx) | Copy + composition; horizon wash wrapper |
| [`homepage/components/BenefitCards.tsx`](homepage/components/BenefitCards.tsx) | Copy; honest chips (no fake counts) |
| [`homepage/components/ProductScreenshot.tsx`](homepage/components/ProductScreenshot.tsx) | Atmosphere cleanup; section copy |
| [`homepage/components/ReassuranceStrip.tsx`](homepage/components/ReassuranceStrip.tsx) / [`ProofFooter.tsx`](homepage/components/ProofFooter.tsx) | Merge into one trust component (edit one, thin the other or fold into it) |
| [`homepage/components/CTA.tsx`](homepage/components/CTA.tsx) | Statement close; reduce search duplication |
| [`homepage/app/globals.css`](homepage/app/globals.css) | Horizon wash, trust band, section band tints (`paper` / `paper-2`); reactivate restrained orange search glow if useful |
| [`homepage/components/Header.tsx`](homepage/components/Header.tsx) | Nav labels only if section anchors/copy change |

**Out of scope:** panel/app chrome, `how-it-works` / `pricing` (already aligned), regenerating `design.md`, changing `design-tokens.css` unless a missing named token is required (then mirror into [`relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css`](relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css)).

**Do not delete** production components without confirmation; prefer fold/edit over removing files.

## Implementation constraints

- All colors/fonts via tokens (`var(--color-*)`, `var(--font-*)`) — no mid-render hex improvisation.
- Mobile: verify 320 / 375 / 414 / 768 — no horizontal scroll; CTAs single-line where possible.
- Motion: existing fade-up / reveal; `prefers-reduced-motion` opacity-only.
- Cards allowed for interactive benefit/board chrome; hero stays non-card (copy + bird + search).
- Follow Hallmark redesign safety: existing routes and search wiring stay.

## Done when

- First viewport reads as one Warm Horizon composition (brand + one H1 + lede + CTAs + bird/horizon + search).
- Section rhythm is open feature-stack, not a dashboard of equal-weight blocks.
- Copy matches open-door tone; no invented metrics.
- Tokens unchanged in spirit; sibling marketing pages still feel like the same site.
- `npm run build` in `homepage/` succeeds.

+# Homepage narrative and design redesign
+
+## Product and brand position
+
+The homepage must explain a complete product, not display a set of UI blocks.
+
+Kuchup is for international backend and software engineers trying to relocate
+within Europe. Its useful distinction is that it joins three jobs that are
+usually fragmented:
+
+1. **Discover** relevant openings from company career pages.
+2. **Track** decisions and application status in one personal board.
+3. **Prepare** a job-specific CV and cover letter with the user approving the
+   final material.
+
+The brand meaning stays intact: the bird represents movement; the open sky and
+horizon represent a new country and opportunity; navy supplies trust and
+orange marks forward action. The copy should feel hopeful and practical, not
+touristic, corporate, or overly cute.
+
+[`design.md`](design.md) remains the locked visual source of truth:
+modern-minimal, Warm Horizon, Lexend + Manrope, ivory paper, sky supporting
+surfaces, navy structure, and restrained orange action.
+
+## Current homepage diagnosis
+
+The first section already communicates the category and exposes real search.
+Everything after it loses momentum:
+
+- “What you get” is three generic feature cards without a persuasive story.
+- The board mockup is shown before visitors understand why it matters.
+- Thin reassurance and proof rows feel appended rather than earned.
+- The same search form appears again at the end.
+- The page omits important conversion answers already available elsewhere:
+  workflow, country coverage, full product scope, access model, and whether
+  Kuchup applies automatically.
+- The page therefore reads like a preview, while the product is an end-to-end
+  relocation job-search workspace.
+
+## Content strategy
+
+### Audience
+
+Primary: an experienced international software engineer considering Germany,
+the Netherlands, the UK, Portugal, Ireland, or another supported market.
+
+Their concerns, in order:
+
+1. Are these roles genuinely relevant to relocation?
+2. Is the data fresh and sourced from real company career pages?
+3. Can I control a growing application pipeline without another spreadsheet?
+4. Can this help me prepare a credible application without inventing claims?
+5. Will it apply without my permission or misuse my documents?
+6. What can I use now, and what requires access?
+
+### Message hierarchy
+
+- **Promise:** one calm path from finding a relocation-friendly role to being
+  ready to apply.
+- **Reason to believe:** direct company-career-page ingestion, support for 25
+  ATS types, six-hour refresh cadence, personal status overlay, and gated CV
+  reframing.
+- **Emotional payoff:** less scattered work and more confidence about the next
+  move.
+- **Primary action:** search the live public catalog.
+- **Secondary action:** open/sign in to the board.
+
+No testimonials, employer logos, success rates, role counts, or sponsorship
+guarantees will be invented. “Visa-friendly” describes catalog focus, not a
+guarantee that every opening sponsors every applicant.
+
+## Single-page landing narrative
+
+```mermaid
+flowchart TB
+  Nav[Anchor navigation]
+  Hero[Promise plus live search]
+  Results[Conditional search results]
+  Evidence[Factual evidence rail]
+  Problem[Why relocation search breaks]
+  Journey[Discover Track Prepare]
+  Countries[Explore by country]
+  Steps[How Kuchup works]
+  Access[Free preview and full workspace]
+  Trust[User control and privacy]
+  FAQ[Objection handling]
+  Close[Final board CTA]
+  Footer[Country and product links]
+
+  Nav --> Hero --> Results --> Evidence --> Problem --> Journey --> Countries
+  Countries --> Steps --> Access --> Trust --> FAQ --> Close --> Footer
+```
+
+### 1. Hero — “the whole path, one place”
+
+- Keep the working `SearchBar` as the central product interaction.
+- Keep the bird at hero scale; place it in a restrained sky/horizon
+  composition, not a decorative mascot bubble.
+- Rewrite the headline around the complete journey, not merely aggregation.
+  Direction: **“From the right role to a ready application.”**
+- Lede: Kuchup finds relocation-focused engineering roles, keeps the search
+  organized, and helps prepare a tailored application.
+- Primary action is the live search form; secondary text link opens the board.
+- Search note explains public preview, no sign-in, direct career-page sourcing,
+  and refresh cadence.
+- `SearchResults` remains immediately after the hero and only appears following
+  interaction.
+
+### 2. Evidence rail — factual credibility early
+
+Use three compact, defensible facts rather than vague reassurance:
+
+- **25 ATS types supported**
+- **Catalog refreshed every 6 hours**
+- **Roles sourced from company career pages**
+
+This is proof of method, not a fake social-proof strip.
+
+### 3. Problem section — name the fragmented work
+
+A short, asymmetric narrative explains the real enemy:
+
+- roles scattered across career sites,
+- sponsorship context that is difficult to judge,
+- statuses split between browser tabs and spreadsheets,
+- a master CV reused where a focused application is needed.
+
+The section closes with the bridge: Kuchup turns those disconnected tasks into
+one workflow. It should use typography and a simple path motif, not four more
+cards.
+
+### 4. Product journey — the persuasive core
+
+Build a long feature-stack with three substantial chapters. On desktop, a
+sticky narrative pane is paired with a product view; mobile becomes a normal
+vertical sequence without sticky behavior.
+
+1. **Discover roles worth considering**
+   - Company-level, country-aware catalog.
+   - Search and filtering over direct career-page openings.
+   - Freshness/cadence is visible without overselling sponsorship certainty.
+   - Visual: searchable board/catalog state.
+
+2. **Track every decision**
+   - Looking to apply, applied, awaiting response, rejected, not for me, pinned.
+   - Per-user state stays separate from the shared catalog.
+   - Company workspace keeps positions and documents together.
+   - Visual: real status pills and company card progression.
+
+3. **Prepare with evidence, not invention**
+   - Job-description mirror, master CV, project evidence, cover letter, and PDF.
+   - Explicitly state that the user reviews and approves the result.
+   - Explicitly state that Kuchup does not submit applications automatically.
+   - Visual: compact application workspace/CV pipeline representation.
+
+This replaces the current generic three-card benefits row and the detached
+single board screenshot.
+
+### 5. Countries — make relocation concrete
+
+Add a country exploration band using the existing `countryLinks()` source:
+Germany, Netherlands, UK, Portugal, Ireland, and current custom-country
+behavior. Each country link goes to its existing SEO route.
+
+The section should communicate that country pages are entry points into the
+same workflow, not isolated job-board categories. No hardcoded role counts.
+
+### 6. How it works — compress the full workflow onto home
+
+Bring a concise version of the existing How It Works content into the landing
+page:
+
+1. Browse the public catalog.
+2. Sign in to save and track.
+3. Prepare a role-specific application.
+4. Return to a catalog refreshed every six hours.
+
+Keep `/how-it-works` as a deeper route and SEO target; the homepage summary
+links to it.
+
+### 7. Access — explain what visitors can use
+
+Bring the essential access model from Pricing onto the homepage without
+pretending a settled paid price exists:
+
+- **Public preview:** search, country discovery, and sample company results.
+- **Workspace access:** full board, tracking, company documents, CV/cover
+  letter workflow.
+- Label unlaunched commercial details honestly as “pricing to be announced.”
+
+Keep `/pricing` as the detailed route. Do not repeat the current “coming soon”
+language in several places.
+
+### 8. Trust principles — control is part of the product
+
+Use a calm statement section, not generic icon reassurance:
+
+- Kuchup does not mass-apply or submit on the user’s behalf.
+- Tailored material is reviewed before saving/use.
+- Personal tracking is private to the user.
+- Job data and personal workflow have distinct roles.
+
+### 9. FAQ — answer conversion blockers
+
+Use native accessible disclosure elements for five questions:
+
+- Does every listed role guarantee visa sponsorship?
+- Where do the jobs come from?
+- How fresh is the catalog?
+- Does Kuchup apply for me?
+- What is available without signing in?
+
+Answers must match actual behavior and existing docs.
+
+### 10. Final CTA and footer
+
+- End with a large Warm Horizon statement: the move is complex; the search
+  does not have to be.
+- One orange action opens the board; one quiet link returns to search.
+- Do not render a second search form.
+- Footer retains product, country, and source links, but gains a one-sentence
+  brand descriptor so “KUCHUP” is meaningful at the close.
+
+## Visual system applied to the new story
+
+- Alternate warm-paper and open-sky section bands to make the long page legible.
+- Use negative space and hairline rules rather than enclosing every idea in a
+  card.
+- Reserve cards for product UI, access choices, and FAQ disclosures.
+- Use one Tier-A horizon motif through the hero and closing section; reuse the
+  existing Tier-B bird SVG.
+- Use asymmetry in the problem and country sections; use sticky feature-stack
+  pacing only for the three product chapters.
+- Orange remains under five percent of a viewport and marks actions/progress.
+- Motion supports hierarchy: hero entrance, chapter state transitions, and
+  restrained disclosure behavior. Respect reduced motion.
+
+## Component and file plan
+
+### Modify
+
+- [`homepage/app/page.tsx`](homepage/app/page.tsx): replace the current section
+  assembly with the full single-page narrative while preserving
+  `SearchFlowProvider`.
+- [`homepage/components/Hero.tsx`](homepage/components/Hero.tsx): new complete
+  promise, hero composition, and single live search.
+- [`homepage/components/Header.tsx`](homepage/components/Header.tsx): anchor-led
+  homepage navigation for Product, Countries, How it works, and Access; retain
+  route-safe links and sign-in.
+- [`homepage/components/CTA.tsx`](homepage/components/CTA.tsx): convert from a
+  duplicate search card into the final statement CTA.
+- [`homepage/components/Footer.tsx`](homepage/components/Footer.tsx): add the
+  short brand descriptor and align links with the new narrative.
+- [`homepage/app/globals.css`](homepage/app/globals.css): long-page bands,
+  horizon motif, sticky feature-stack, product-view compositions, FAQ states,
+  and responsive fallbacks.
+
+### Create
+
+- [`homepage/components/EvidenceRail.tsx`](homepage/components/EvidenceRail.tsx)
